description: Machine-readable registry of agent platforms (drivers/executors/engines) for Metis OS. Human companion: multi-provider-agent-framework.md. Consumed by scripts/platform-parity-check.py (read-only drift/parity report). The neutral core is the single source of truth; each platform is a thin adapter declared here. Paths are repo-relative unless prefixed with ~.
tier1PacksNote: Tiered-context architecture (docs/process/tiered-context-architecture.md): the on-demand Tier-1 packs. Each must (a) have its canonical doc present and (b) be referenced by the shared neutral Router (packRouter = AGENTS.md), so every provider that reads AGENTS.md routes to the same pack set. platform-parity-check.py enforces both.

platforms.notes: Reference implementation. Lifecycle enforced by settings.json hooks (session-init, prompt-guard, stop, statusline).

Codex CLI custom prompts (~/.codex/prompts) provide legacy slash adapters; reusable workflows are exposed as repo skills via .agents/skills, symlinked to ClaudeCode/skills. Memory unified to ClaudeCode/memory via mirror symlink. Native Codex hooks are configured in ~/.codex/hooks.json for Metis guard/context parity; trusted command hashes live in ~/.codex/config.toml and must be re-approved when hook commands change. Repo launch policy lives in .codex/config.toml + docs/process/codex-launch-policy.md; mirror.py merges only approval_policy=never and sandbox_mode=danger-full-access into local config, and launchers should pass --sandbox danger-full-access --ask-for-approval never.

Local Ollama lanes invoked via scripts/dispatch. Executor role: receives sub-tasks, returns output; does not own session lifecycle, so no command surface is required. Jay is the sole Ollama host for qwen3-coder:30b; Jarry is secondary/on-demand and routes Ollama to Jay. Jarry currently runs OpenClaw sandbox mode off because Docker is unavailable there, so keep Jarry lanes proposal-only unless sandboxing is restored and verified.

Successor to Gemini CLI, which Google is deprecating for free/Pro/Ultra individual tiers on 2026-06-18 (so do NOT build against Gemini CLI). Antigravity CLI supports skills/hooks/subagents/extensions at launch and would run on Ant's existing Google AI tier. ON-HOLD: Ant will only adopt it if usage is BUNDLED in the tier he already pays for, not metered pay-per-API — confirm the billing model before bring-up. Not open-source; expect rough edges right after the transition.

GitHub Copilot CLI (local driver) + cloud coding agent (issue->PR in Actions); both read AGENTS.md (our neutral entrypoint), so near-zero glue, and it's the most GitHub-native fit for our issue/PR/lease flow. Cheapest paid agent at $10/mo Pro. BLOCKED: new Pro/Pro+/Max signups paused since 2026-04-20. scripts/copilot-signup-watch.py (daily via ant.copilot-signup-watch.plist) pings Discord when signups reopen or plans change; build the adapter then.

$20/mo agentic IDE with a headless CLI (cursor-agent) that reads AGENTS.md. DECLINED for now: its differentiator is the editor surface, which is redundant with Claude Code + Codex CLI for a headless, multi-session framework. Revisit only if Ant wants a hands-on IDE.
